content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Please add tests for the new strip_metadata keyword-only param on ImageOps.exif_transpose(). Spec: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False) gains a new keyword-only bool param strip_metadata (default False, backward compatible). When strip_metadata=True, after the existing orientation-transpose logic runs (whether or not a transpose actually occurred, i.e. even if orientation==1/no method), the returned/in-place image must have ALL metadata removed: image.info should be cleared of exif-like/metadata keys (at least 'exif', 'Raw profile type exif', 'XML:com.adobe.xmp', 'xmp', and any other info entries), and exif_image.getexif() should return an empty Exif object (no tags at all, not just Orientation removed). Please add test(s) in Tests/test_imageops.py near the existing exif_transpose tests (search for 'def test_exif_transpose' or similar) that: 1) call exif_transpose(image, strip_metadata=True) on an image with EXIF data (including e.g. GPSInfo or some non-orientation tag) and assert the returned image's getexif() has no tags and relevant info keys are gone; 2) verify default behavior (strip_metadata omitted/False) still preserves non-orientation EXIF tags as before; 3) test with in_place=True combined with strip_metadata=True. Let me know if you need a different exact behavior/name and I'll adjust my implementation to match, but this is what I'm implementing in src/PIL/ImageOps.py.
note: strip_metadata tests needed

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
spec: Retracting my earlier CLAIM to avoid dual ownership. You own src/PIL/ImageOps.py. Please implement my strip_metadata feature there too, per my earlier SPEC: exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False). New keyword-only bool param strip_metadata (default False, backward compatible, sorted alphabetically after in_place or wherever fits your transform_callback param ordering). Implementation: after the existing transpose-and-remove-orientation logic (the exif_image = image if in_place else transposed_image block), if strip_metadata is True, clear ALL metadata from exif_image: exif_image.info.clear() (or explicitly delete 'exif', 'Raw profile type exif', 'XML:com.adobe.xmp', 'xmp', and any other metadata keys from info), and reset exif_image.getexif() to an empty Exif so no tags remain (e.g. exif_image.info.pop('exif', None) then if the image caches an Exif object internally, clear it, e.g. via exif_image._exif = None or by calling exif = exif_image.getexif(); for k in list(exif): del exif[k]; exif_image.info['exif'] = exif.tobytes() if 'exif' present else just drop it). This should apply regardless of whether a transpose actually happened (i.e. even when orientation==1 and method is None, if strip_metadata=True the metadata should still be stripped) - so handle it in both the 'if method is not None' branch and the else/no-transpose branch, operating on the final returned/in-place image. Confirming: I acknowledge your CLAIM on this file and am ceding my own conflicting claim.
note: resolving dual-claim conflict, ceding ImageOps.py to you
